overview: Tensions escalate in the Iran-US conflict with recent Iranian missile strikes and US President Trump's vow to continue military action, causing spikes in oil prices and concerns over disruptions to India's internet and higher education data centers. Meanwhile, AI adoption grows in education and business sectors, and local Boston initiatives focus on policy and economic impacts amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.
podcast_script: Good morning. Today’s top story centers on the escalating conflict between Iran and the US. Recent Iranian missile strikes targeted US naval facilities in Bahrain, prompting President Trump to deliver a prime-time address promising to "finish the job" soon. This rhetoric has driven oil prices higher and raised concerns about potential disruptions to India's internet infrastructure and higher education data centers due to the conflict’s wider impact. In other news, the US has lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, signaling shifts in diplomatic relations. On the technology front, a new survey reveals widespread AI use at California State universities despite skepticism, with many students reconsidering their majors due to AI’s influence. Experts also discuss legal compliance strategies for managed service providers adapting to AI tools like Copilot. Locally in Boston, the Rappaport Institute has launched a $2 million initiative to address regional policy challenges, while businesses assess the economic impact of the World Cup. Finally, NASA’s Artemis II mission continues with minor technical issues, as four astronauts embark on the first crewed lunar mission in half a century. That’s the briefing for today. Stay informed and have a great day.
